description_long: This object is a realistic crustacean, resembling a freshwater crayfish or small lobster, with a segmented, dark brown to reddish exoskeleton. Its body is divided into a robust cephalothorax and a flexible, banded abdomen, ending in a broad, fan-shaped tail. Prominent claws (chelae) extend from the front, flanked by multiple walking legs on each side. Two long, slender antennae project forward from the head, accompanied by smaller sensory appendages. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the cephalothorax, providing a wide field of vision. The surface appears hard and slightly glossy, with subtle textural ridges and color variations across the shell.
description: A detailed, dark reddish-brown crustacean with segmented body, large claws, long antennae, and a hard, glossy shell. The anatomy includes a cephalothorax, abdomen, and fan-shaped tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crustacean, highlighting the claws, walking legs, and long antennae.
description_view_1: Top-down view displaying the segmented back, symmetrical leg arrangement, and broad tail fan.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, revealing the other profile of the claws, legs, and antennae.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail fan and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs and claws.
